text.title: Quality Attributes Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Quality Attributes Analysis` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every NFR is expressed as a 6-part quality-attribute scenario: source, stimulus, artefact, environment, response, response-measure. Free-text NFRs = REJECT.
Every leaf scenario carries (Importance, Difficulty) ∈ {L,M,H} pair. Unscored leaves = INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Without scoring, prioritisation collapses to opinion.

rule.statement: Every (H,H) scenario MUST have an executable fitness function (load test, chaos drill, security scan, perf benchmark) wired to CI/CD. (H,H) without test = REJECT.
Each scenario maps to ≥ 1 ISO/IEC 25010:2023 quality characteristic (reliability, performance efficiency, security, maintainability, etc.).
rule.rationale: Standard mapping makes audit + compliance straightforward.

rule.statement: ATAM tradeoff analysis MUST identify ≥ 2 sensitivity points + ≥ 1 tradeoff point per decision. 'No tradeoffs' = REJECT.
Every scenario has a named owner accountable for the fitness function staying green.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
